item.title: Tesla shares rally as profits rebound from last year’s lows
item.description: Net income rises 17% but is still second-weakest figure in five years

item.title: Builder.ai founder Sachin Dev Duggal accused of receiving siphoned funds
item.description: Indian authorities name UK start-up founder in criminal complaint over ties to collapsed electronics group

item.title: Crypto billionaire Justin Sun sues Trump family’s World Liberty Financial
item.description: Fraud claims come amid escalating feud between the Trump venture and one of its biggest supporters

item.title: Chelsea’s US owners sack another head coach
item.description: Liam Rosenior serves just three months of a five-and-a-half-year contract in London club’s latest managerial reshuffle

item.title: CVC-led group closes in on £1bn Standard Life pensions deal
item.description: Consortium, which includes Prudential Financial, would help fund buyouts of defined-benefit schemes

item.title: EU rethinks opposition to Arctic oil and gas drilling
item.description: U-turn by Brussels would be bitterly contested by environmental groups

item.title: Drahi’s €20bn SFR deal is a masterclass in hardball negotiation
item.description: Rivals may prefer to just watch him get into ever hotter water but buying his company outright gives them more certainty

item.title: Ex-RBS banker sentenced over bribery linked to GRG scandal
item.description: Stuart Holloway is the only person to be convicted over the bank’s infamous SME restructuring unit

item.title: US may extend $500mn in rescue funding to Spirit Airlines
item.description: Money would come with equity warrants that could make government majority owner of struggling discount carrier

item.title: AI should not drive today’s interest rate decisions
item.description: How the technology will affect prices is still uncertain

item.title: German chancellor Merz defends industry against EU climate policy
item.description: Divisions over response to Middle East energy crisis evident at annual discussions held in Berlin

item.title: Ecocide is a crime against humanity
item.description: The ICC and its member states must treat environmental destruction as seriously as other international crimes

item.title: Chinese EV maker Nio faces multimillion-dollar claim over patents
item.description: Nio says accusations that it infringed IP covering technology from defunct Israeli EV pioneer Better Place are ‘unfounded’

item.title: Energy traders dig in for a long Iran war
item.description: Vitol, Trafigura and rivals expand credit lines in anticipation of prolonged disruption to global oil and gas flows

item.title: Russia to suspend Kazakh oil flows through key pipeline supplying Berlin
item.description: Plan will hit Rosneft refinery that supplies most of German capital’s petrol, kerosene and heating fuel

item.title: AI robot outplays humans in table tennis milestone
item.description: Sony’s ‘Ace’ defeats elite players, highlighting how AI is improving machines’ abilities to interact with people

item.title: EQT makes improved £9.7bn offer for Intertek
item.description: Previous bid for FTSE 100 group from Swedish private equity firm rejected

item.title: EQT warns AI fears will stall sales of private equity software stakes
item.description: Investor concerns that technology could hit companies’ business models will derail exits, says Swedish group

item.title: Switzerland hits UBS with proposed $20bn capital increase
item.description: Federal Council sets out plans for banking reform after months of lobbying by country’s biggest lender

item.title: UK lenders urge judges to block mass motor finance lawsuits
item.description: Case in Court of Appeal will help shape whether motorists can make claims outside of £9.1bn redress scheme

item.title: Ukraine miner Ferrexpo warns it could run out of cash by August
item.description: London-listed company’s shares plunge as it considers capital raise of at least $100mn

item.title: Eni founder’s heir clashes with Meloni government over name use
item.description: Italian government’s ‘Mattei plan’ for Africa is part of Rome’s efforts to curb migration

item.title: Boeing regains delivery lead over Airbus as revenues jump
item.description: US aircraft maker narrows losses and posts widest gap over European rival since 2018

item.title: Apple controls the tech sector’s Strait of Hormuz
item.description: It may have stumbled in the AI race but the company’s new CEO will find it still has distinct advantages

item.title: House prices in London’s wealthiest boroughs suffer double-digit decline
item.description: Westminster and Kensington and Chelsea property valuations sink close to 2013 levels

item.title: UK dilutes post-crisis ‘fit and proper’ rules for financial services staff
item.description: Changes are first stage in wider shake-up by Financial Conduct Authority

item.title: Balls, boats and billionaires: Como 1907’s lifestyle brand aspirations
item.description: Once, Como 1907 was just a football club. Now, its owners are trying to build a luxury ecosystem around its glamorous location

item.title: Who will pay the rising costs of disaster insurance?
item.description: Governments are grappling with a fiendish policy dilemma

item.title: Sam’s Club launches one-hour US delivery with shoppers in ‘on-demand phase’
item.description: Walmart-owned warehouse chain will compete for market share from rivals Amazon and Costco

item.title: France and Germany extend deadline to try to save joint fighter jet
item.description: Mediators have until April 28 to overcome power struggle between Airbus and Dassault Aviation
